concepts.name: Probability
concepts.short_definition: The likelihood of an event occurring.

concepts.name: Event
concepts.short_definition: A specific outcome or set of outcomes in a probability experiment.

concepts.name: Sample Space
concepts.short_definition: The set of all possible outcomes of an experiment.

concepts.name: Independent Events
concepts.short_definition: Events where the occurrence of one does not affect the probability of the other.

concepts.name: Dependent Events
concepts.short_definition: Events where the occurrence of one affects the probability of the other.

concepts.name: Conditional Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of an event A occurring given that event B has already occurred.

concepts.name: Joint Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of two or more events occurring simultaneously.

concepts.name: Marginal Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of a single event occurring, without considering other events.

concepts.name: Addition Rule
concepts.short_definition: Used to calculate the probability of the union of two events.

concepts.name: Multiplication Rule
concepts.short_definition: Used to calculate the probability of the intersection of two events.

concepts.name: Law of Total Probability
concepts.short_definition: States that the probability of an event is the sum of its conditional probabilities over a partition of the sample space.

concepts.name: Bayes' Theorem
concepts.short_definition: A formula that describes the probability of an event based on prior knowledge of conditions that might be related to the event.

concepts.name: Prior Probability
concepts.short_definition: The initial probability of an event before new evidence is considered.

concepts.name: Posterior Probability
concepts.short_definition: The updated probability of an event after new evidence is considered.

concepts.name: Likelihood
concepts.short_definition: The probability of observing the data given a particular hypothesis or parameter value.

concepts.name: Partition
concepts.short_definition: A set of mutually exclusive and exhaustive events that cover the entire sample space.

concepts.name: Contingency Table
concepts.short_definition: A table that displays the frequency distribution of variables, often used for conditional probability calculations.

concepts.name: Tree Diagram
concepts.short_definition: A visual tool used to represent sequential events and their probabilities, useful for conditional probability.

concepts.name: Set Theory
concepts.short_definition: Mathematical study of sets, fundamental for understanding events and sample spaces.

concepts.name: Union of Events
concepts.short_definition: The event that either A or B or both occur.

concepts.name: Intersection of Events
concepts.short_definition: The event that both A and B occur.

concepts.name: Complement of an Event
concepts.short_definition: The event that A does not occur.

concepts.name: Mutually Exclusive Events
concepts.short_definition: Events that cannot occur at the same time.

concepts.name: Random Variable
concepts.short_definition: A variable whose value is a numerical outcome of a random phenomenon.

concepts.name: Probability Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A function that describes the likelihood of obtaining the possible values that a random variable can assume.

concepts.name: Conditional Distribution
concepts.short_definition: The probability distribution of a random variable given that another random variable takes a specific value.

concepts.name: Statistical Independence
concepts.short_definition: A property of random variables where the joint probability distribution is the product of their marginal distributions.

concepts.name: Statistical Dependence
concepts.short_definition: The absence of statistical independence between random variables.

concepts.name: Causal Inference
concepts.short_definition: The process of determining whether a relationship between variables is causal, often relying on conditional probability.

concepts.name: Diagnostic Testing
concepts.short_definition: Using conditional probability to evaluate the accuracy of medical tests.

concepts.name: Sensitivity
concepts.short_definition: The probability that a test correctly identifies a condition when it is present (True Positive Rate).

concepts.name: Specificity
concepts.short_definition: The probability that a test correctly identifies a condition when it is absent (True Negative Rate).

concepts.name: False Positive Rate
concepts.short_definition: The probability that a test incorrectly indicates a condition when it is absent.

concepts.name: False Negative Rate
concepts.short_definition: The probability that a test incorrectly indicates a condition is absent when it is present.

concepts.name: Predictive Value
concepts.short_definition: The probability that a person with a positive or negative test result actually has or does not have the condition.

concepts.name: Chain Rule of Probability
concepts.short_definition: A generalization of the multiplication rule for any number of events.

concepts.name: Independence of Multiple Events
concepts.short_definition: The condition where the occurrence of any subset of events does not affect the probability of the remaining events.

concepts.name: Conditional Independence
concepts.short_definition: Two events are conditionally independent given a third event if their conditional probabilities are independent.

concepts.name: Stochastic Process
concepts.short_definition: A process involving a sequence of random variables, where conditional probabilities play a key role in transitions.
